notes: Lesson 22 Part 2 (act 130), 'Largest Rectangle in a Histogram', 77.66s, 6 cues. heights=[2,1,5,6,2,3], largest = 5x2 = 10 (bars index 2,3, min height 5, width 2). New arrays.py histogram/recolor_bar + rect_overlay idiom + the monotonic-stack column (increasing heights). Three iterations: (1) authored + manim -s smoke all 6 cues READ -> caught a REAL geometry bug: histogram().shift() moved the bars but not the stored hist.xs, so every overlay rectangle/brace was 1.4u right of its bars (cue04 winning 5x2 rect was off the h5/h6 bars) -> baked the offset into hist.xs; (2) re-smoke -> fixed cue03 left/right label collision (shortened to 'left = bar 1'/'right = i4' + subtitles) and cue01/cue02 formula overlaps (moved 6x1=6 inside the tall bar, moved height=5 to clear space); (3) full -ql render + READ start/mid/end of every cue from the muxed mp4 confirming overlay alignment. Per-cue checklist (increasing-stack invariant, shorter-wall triggers pop, height=popped bar's own height, width=nearest-shorter-left..current-index=4-1-1=2, area 5x2=10 beats 6x1=6, sentinel flush, safe-area guard, chunk timing all pinned): all pass. All rectangles now align to their bars; all formulas correct + legible.
